indication: Abdominal pain and constipation, evaluate for obstruction
technique: Single AP supine radiograph of the abdomen was obtained.

findings.bowelGasPattern: Mildly distended loops of colon with stool burden throughout the colon, most prominent in the ascending and transverse colon. No dilated loops of small bowel. No air-fluid levels to suggest obstruction.
findings.calcifications: Atherosclerotic calcification of the abdominal aorta. Pelvic phleboliths. No radiopaque urinary tract calculi.
findings.kidneys: Renal outlines are not well delineated on plain radiograph. No large renal calculi.
findings.softTissues: No free intraperitoneal air. Surgical clips in the right upper quadrant consistent with prior cholecystectomy.
findings.osseous: Degenerative changes of the lumbar spine with disc space narrowing at L4-L5 and L5-S1. No acute fracture or suspicious lytic lesion.

impression: Significant stool burden throughout the colon consistent with constipation.
Atherosclerotic calcification of the abdominal aorta.
comparison: No prior KUB available for comparison.
